ol.li: Double-Compile Hazard: The mutex protection in compiler ensures that concurrent optimization requests for the same function do not lead to redundant codegen or race conditions on the entry pointer.

Memory Ordering (Release/Acquire):
Store-Release: Guarantees that the instructions written to native_ptr by the Compiler are committed to memory/global-observation before the jit_entry pointer becomes non-null.
Load-Acquire: Guarantees that the Worker does not read instructions from the code buffer until it has successfully read the updated jit_entry.

Instruction Cache (ICache) Invalidation:
On x86, atomic_store is usually sufficient for cross-modifying code due to strong TSO.
On ARM/AArch64, the Compiler must execute ISB (Instruction Synchronization Barrier) and DSB (Data Synchronization Barrier) or call __clear_cache() before unlocking.

g.title: Struct layout: [mutex_t lock (40B) | void* jit_entry (8B) | uint32_t call_count (4B)]

text.tspan: Machine code emitted to RX buffer
Ensures all previous writes to code buffer
are visible to other cores before pointer update
Prevents speculative reads of code buffer
before the jit_entry pointer is verified

g.title: Struct layout: [mutex_t lock (40B) | void* jit_entry (8B) | uint32_t call_count (4B)]
